feed.title: AO3 - Merlin/Arthur Pendragon (Merlin) (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Merlin/Arthur Pendragon (Merlin) on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: it's not my fire, but was forged in flame
entry.summary:
Author: elfesetamour

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 66,959Chapters: 25/?Kudos: 79

Summary: Unable to heal from Camelot’s greatest loss, Merlin is haunted by nightmares—or something more sinister—of Arthur. But, despite the undeniable resemblance, it’s impossible that the Arthur in his dreams is the same one he’d held in his arms as he died. Searching through the Mist, Merlin leaves his old life behind in search of a new destiny, which brings him to Paris, 1643. In a land of feuds and a time of revolution, among old faces as well as new, Merlin searches for meaning side by side with Arthur again. But whether he can come to terms with their differences, or accept his feelings for this new Arthur, all the while protecting him, may be a greater challenge than he signed up for. "That was Arthur, standing right in front of him. Yes, he wasn’t completely the same but that was him-no mistake. A scar visible on his cheek, and that beard covering his face couldn’t fool Merlin. He knew Arthur better than that. " Merlin’s magic takes him on a journey through time to Musketeer Era France, where he meets Arthur, the Captain of the King’s Red Guard. Ruthless, bloodthirsty, and emotionally closed off, Merlin seems unable to deny anything this new Arthur asks of him.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: The Deepest Hurt
entry.summary:
Author: gloriask

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,672Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 25

Summary: Arthur had always said that a warrior being wounded in battle was nothing more than the natural order of things. It was the cost of knighthood. The price of loyalty. The proof of courage. From the time he was old enough to hold a wooden sword, he had been taught to wear injury like a badge. Blood on steel, bruises beneath armor. A prince did not flinch. A future king did not falter. And a warrior certainly did not fear wounds, no matter how deep. He had believed it until—

entry.title: Game Of Convincing
entry.summary:
Author: OfTintagel

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,140Chapters: 1/7Kudos: 1

Summary: When Merlin gets fatally stabbed, protecting Arthur in the woods- and doesnt seem to have time to even get to Gaius. And Gaius might be too far but the Disir is that close and Arthur knows they can heal him. So he makes a decision. The Disir accepts to heal Merlin but they have a condition. They must convince some people- dead or alive that Merlin deserves to be saved. Secrets starts to get unraveled.

entry.title: Destiny's Hold
entry.summary:
Author: give_me_a_croissant

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 12,068Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 44

Summary: Starting in the middle of S5E5. Arthur is faced with an impossible decision. Accept the old religion back into his kingdom and save Sir Mordred's life, or continue his campaign against sorcery and condemn the young knight to certain death. After receiving advice from Merlin, Arthur decides to do something that will forever change the fate of Camelot, and hopefully his own destiny. OR What if Arthur accepted magic back into his kingdom to save Mordred, and then he quickly finds out that Merlin, who has been by his side through all of this, is a sorcerer himself? How does Arthur's decision with the Disir change his and Merlin's destiny?

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: When the Sunlight Dims
entry.summary:
Author: thesugarplumfairy

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 15,690Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 44

Summary: After being gone from Camelot for three years, Merlin couldn't care less about the Kingdom or it's King. When he has to go back to right a wrong, he plans to do what he must and then return home. Of course, King Arthur musn't know he's in town, which is fine because he'll only be there for a night. Right?

entry.title: THE WIZARD BEHIND THE THRONE?
entry.summary:
Author: Rita Skeeter (JeniferJuni)

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 5,763Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 4

Summary: 𝐌𝐞𝐫𝐥𝐢𝐧’𝐬 𝐌𝐮𝐠𝐠𝐥𝐞 𝐏𝐮𝐩𝐩𝐞𝐭, 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐏𝐫𝐢𝐦𝐞 𝐌𝐢𝐧𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫,𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐒𝐜𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐚𝐥 𝐓𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐑𝐨𝐜𝐤𝐬 𝐁𝐨𝐭𝐡 𝐖𝐨𝐫𝐥𝐝𝐬 𝐁𝐲 𝐑𝐢𝐭𝐚 𝐒𝐤𝐞𝐞𝐭𝐞𝐫 𝐒𝐩𝐞𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐈𝐧𝐯𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐠𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐫𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐩𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭, 𝐃𝐚𝐢𝐥𝐲 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐩𝐡𝐞𝐭 𝐏𝐮𝐛𝐥𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐞𝐝: 𝟏𝟒 𝐉𝐮𝐧𝐞 𝟏𝟗𝟖𝟖

entry.title: Forever Is My Tomorrow
entry.summary:
Author: Mahogany_Rust

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 467,377Chapters: 49/80Kudos: 1,649

Summary: It had actually been a long time since Hiccup had thought about how he would die.When he was young, every sunrise was another gamble to see if he would get himself eaten by a dragon or somehow manage to fall off one of Berk’s many cliffs.Perhaps Winter would finally choose to break his wiry body with illness or he would catch an unfortunate stray battle axe.Yet here he was, 21, about to die in the wrong place (not a bad age to reach in the Archipelago when you’re Hiccup Haddock, let's be honest). But that was the thing wasn’t it? Hiccup made plenty of marks all in the wrong places. What if one of those marks was in the future? On one unsuspecting spirit’s affections?---- FIMT CINEMATIC TRAILER

entry.title: The Naivety of Knights
entry.summary:
Author: DukeySquid

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 9,784Chapters: 2/2Kudos: 7

Summary: Mordred attends his first hunting trip with the knights. While around the fire one evening the topics of sex and love get brought up. When proven wrong about their assumptions of Mordred’s innocences they are shocked to find out that not only has Mordred had sex before, but that he might know much more about love than any of them do. Features a bonus overly sappy happy ending chapter.

entry.summary:
Author: DragonTraining101

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 78,177Chapters: 36/?Kudos: 82

Summary: Merlin attempts to take his own life, but instead sets in motion a series of events the gods did not plan for. The gods are now scrambling to force a new prophecy. Faces from the past and present blur together as Merlin tries to save the world he wanted to leave.

entry.title: The Language of Flowers
entry.summary:
Author: arduine

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 35,983Chapters: 12/15Kudos: 58

Summary: In which: there are flowers, feelings, fantasy, and forgiveness -- although not necessarily in that order. or For as long as Arthur could remember, the importance of tradition had been impressed upon him. So, it was only natural for the prince to assume using Camelot’s oldest would be the perfect way of expressing his intentions. This is, of course, a terrible decision.

entry.title: The Legend
entry.summary:
Author: BlueTortoise137

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 8,914Chapters: 10/?Kudos: 246

Summary: Arthur remembers everything on his twenty first birthday. Merlin isn't there. - “Okay, so let me get this straight,” Arthur said, resisting the urge to bury his face in his hands and scream until the world made sense again. “We’ve all been reborn over a thousand years after our deaths, met one another, seemingly by chance, then all regained our memories on the same day, while wandering around a museum exhibit on our previous lives. Did I miss anything?”

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: A Garden of Forgiveness
entry.summary:
Author: BeautifulFiction

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 14,549Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 258

Summary: 'Merlin, my friend. What –?' 'I need your help hiding a body.' Every man in the antechamber froze, the silence thick and breathless. No one so much as moved as they all stared at the threshold, where the door stood ajar only a fraction, blocking them from view. Yet it was Lancelot's reply, carried on the edge of a weary sigh, that made disbelief stutter in Arthur's chest. 'Again?' ****** A fragment of overheard conversation leads Arthur and his knights deep into the Darkling Woods, where the truth about Merlin's magic finally comes out.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: The Weight of What He Carries
entry.summary:
Author: KillingButterlies88745

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,990Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 8

Summary: When Arthur starts sensing magic, he assumes the worst. After all, magic is evil. Magic destroys kingdoms. Magic killed his mother. At least, that's what he's always been told. But as Merlin helps him investigate the truth behind his strange new ability, Arthur begins to realise that magic, and perhaps everything he believes about it, is far more complicated than he ever imagined.
